rulings.Case Title: FLICK, INC. VS. MING CHAO LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Flick Inc.'S Motion To Compel On Dec 16 2024 Discovery Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 2, 2025 Line 1. RESPONDENT MING LIU's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Flick Inc.'S Motion To Compel On Dec 16 2024 Discovery Hearing. Respondent Ming Chao Liu's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Flick Inc.'s Motion To Compel On Dec 16, 2024, Discovery Hearing is DENIED. Respondent fails to identify a basis for her motion. Liberally construed, the motion appears to be a motion for reconsideration. Respondent's motion is denied as procedurally infirm. Respondent has not shown that he made his motion within ten days of service of notice of entry of the challenged order. (See Code Civil Procedure, section 1008(a).) Further, a party seeking reconsideration must demonstrate diligence. (Baldwin v. Home Savings of America (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 1192, 1200.) Respondent here has not done so. As an independent grounds for denial, the court finds the motion it without substantive support. Respondent fails to demonstrate "new or different facts, circumstances, or law" to warrant reconsideration. (See Code Civil Procedure, section 1008(a).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHAK LUNG SIU VS. MING LIAN ZHOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 2, 2025 Line 3. DEFENDANT HUI DA CONSTRUCTION INC.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Cross-Complaint. Defendant and Cross-complainant Hui Da Construction Inc.'s Motion for Leave to File Amended Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. Defendant and Cross-complainant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 428.50(c). This section provides, "A party shall obtain leave of court to file any cross-complaint except one filed within the time specified in subdivision (a) or (b). Leave may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." The inquiry, thus, is interest of justice." "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.) Courts are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint "at any stage of the proceedings, up to and including trial," absent prejudice to the adverse party. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) Plaintiff and Cross-defendant Chak Lung Siu has not demonstrated cognizable bad faith or prejudice. To the extent the amendment may expand issues or give rise to additional discovery, trial is more than six months away. In any event, the parties are free to seek a trial continuance on these grounds. Whether the amendments might give rise to a motion to dismiss or strike is not a question at this stage. Such matters are better presented by motion after the amended cross-complaint is filed. (See Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 760 ["the better course of action would have been to allow [plaintiff] to amend the complaint and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other proceedings"].) Defendant and Cross-complainant must file the amended complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: VIA LICENSING ALLIANCE LLC VS. HP INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion 1. For Issuance Of A Letter Rogatory To The High Court Of Hong Kong [Unopposed]; And 2. For An Order Modifying Protective Order To Permit Submission To Hong Kong Court [Opposed] **Redacted**
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 2, 2025 Line 9. DEFENDANT HP INC. Motion 1. For Issuance Of A Letter Rogatory To The High Court Of Hong Kong [Unopposed]; And 2. For An Order Modifying Protective Order To Permit Submission To Hong Kong Court [Opposed] **Redacted** Defendant HP Inc.'s Motion for Issuance of a Letter Rogatory and for an Order Modifying Protective Order to Permit Submission to Hong Kong Court is GRATNED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant has established that a letter rogatory is necessary and convenient. (See Code Civil Procedure section 2027.010(e).) Good cause appearing, the requested letter rogatory shall issue. Defendant has not established good cause to support an order modifying the protective order. The Hong Kong court may expect "background documents" and such documents may be "standard." But it is not clear that background documents are necessary necessary. Indeed, this court has already ruled on the need for the discovery. Even if some background documents are necessary, Defendant's request is wildly overbroad and is denied on this basis. The court will not cull the 20 or more exhibits to determine what is reasonably necessary. Nor is it clear that HP Inc. cannot reasonably provide any necessary background without reference to materials the parties deem confidential under the protective order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
